properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Rabbitohs v Broncos at Accor Stadium in Olympic Park. Kick-off at 7.50pm, full time around 9.35pm.

Traffic will be heavy as fans travel to and from the match. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
IVE at Qudos Bank Arena on Saturday 13 June. Show starts at 7.30pm

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you're driving, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.otherAdvice:
Winter Cup Day at Rosehill Gardens Racecourse. First race at 11am, last race from 4.25pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on James Ruse Dr and Hassall St as attendees arrive and leave the racecourse.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads in the area.

roads.mainStreet: James Ruse Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: WestsTigers v Titans at Leichhardt Oval. Kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 6pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on James St, Perry St, Balmain Rd, Mary St and Glover St as attendees travel to and from the match.

A section of Mary Street may close before the game and will reopen when crowds have cleared the area. Traffic will be detoured via Chapel St while the closure is in place.   

Motorists needing access to other venues in the area should use either Maliyawul St or Glover St.

Attendees driving to the game should allow extra travel time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Glover Street and Mary Street

webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

Friday 12 June - After Dark event and street party - traffic changes and parking restrictions

  • From 5pm Fri 12 June to 4am Sat 13 Jun - Full road closure on Robert St between Mullens St and Port Access Rd
  • 8am Temporary no parking zone on southern side of Robert St
  • 4pm Temporary no parking zone on northern side of Robert St

roads.mainStreet: Robert Street

webLinks.linkText: World Cup Live Site - Parramatta Square

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A live site will be set up for the FIFA World Cup men's games from Sunday 14 to Friday 26 June.

The matches will be shown live on the big screen and the site is free to visit:

  • Sunday 14 June: Australia vs Türkiye, kick off 2pm AEST
  • Saturday 20 June: USA v Australia, kick off 5am AEST
  • Friday 26 June: Paraguay v Australia, kick off 12pm AEST

Traffic may be heavier than usual in the area. Motorists should be aware of increased pedestrian activity on roads around Parramatta Square and station.

roads.mainStreet: O'Connell Street, Phillip Street, Macquarie St and George Street

webLinks.linkText: World Cup live site - Darling Harbour

properties.publicTransport: L1 Light rail services run regularly to Exhibition Centre and Paddys Markets within walking distance of Tumbalong Park. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A live site will be open at Tumbalong Park in Darling Harbour from Sunday 14 June to Monday 20 July for the upcoming FIFA World Cup Men's matches.

The matches will be shown live on the big screen and the site is free to visit:

  • Sunday 14 June: Australia vs Türkiye, kick off 2pm AEST
  • Saturday 20 June: USA v Australia, kick off 5am AEST
  • Friday 26 June: Paraguay v Australia, kick off 12pm AEST
  • 9 – 11 July: Quarter-finals, date and time TBC
  • 14 – 15 July: Semi-finals, date and time TBC
  • Mon 20 July: Final, time TBC

Attendees are encouraged to catch public transport as traffic may be heavier than usual.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads throughout the Sydney CBD.

roads.mainStreet: Harbour Street, Darling Drive

webLinks.linkText: TfNSW Vivid Sydney - top tips to plan your trip

properties.publicTransport: Trains will not drop off at Circular Quay from 6pm-11pm on Saturdays. Buses will relocate to Martin Place from 6pm Fridays and 5pm on weekends. Light rail will not run between Circular Quay and Town Hall from 6pm Fridays and 5pm on weekends. Ferries will be busy on weekends and access to wharves at Circular Quay will be different on busy nights – follow signage. For more details and top tips to plan your trip, visit transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Vivid Sydney returns to light up the city for its 16th year across 23 consecutive nights from Friday 22 May to Saturday 13 June.

The city is no place for cars with road closures and clearway parking restrictions in place each night in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Dawes Point and Millers Point between 3pm and midnight daily, and until 1am on Saturday nights.

Drone shows have been cancelled for the remainder of Vivid, visit the website for details.

Road closures:

  • Monday to Thursdays: from 3pm local roads in The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close including sections of Lower Fort St, Merriman St and Rodens Ln. From 5pm additional roads will close including sections of George St, Argyle St and Argyle Pl, Harrington St and Pottinger St.
  • Fridays: additional closures will be in place from 3pm on High St and High Ln. From 5pm additional closures will be in place including all of Argyle St, and sections of Hickson Rd, George St, Essex St, Upper Fort St and Watson Rd. From 6pm roads in Circular Quay including Pitt, Loftus, Young, Albert and Phillip Streets will close.
  • Saturdays: local closures will be in place from 3pm, and major roads in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close from 5pm. Additionally Grosvenor St/Bridge St between York St and Loftus St will be closed - no east/west access via Bridge St during this time. Roads remain closed until 1am. Wheat Rd will close from 7pm to 11pm.
  • 12pm Friday to 7am Monday each weekend, George St will close between Lower Fort St and Hickson Rd for The Rocks Markets. 

Special event clearways will be in effect on most major roads in the city between 3pm and midnight daily during Vivid Sydney. Some clearways will be in effect earlier from 1pm and 2pm on local roads.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: George St, Hickson Rd, Bridge St
roads.suburb: Circular Quay, The Rocks, Barangaroo, Darling Harbour

webLinks.linkText: TfNSW Interactive road closure and clearway map - Parramatta Half Marathon

properties.publicTransport: Light rail services will not run between Parramatta Square and Westmead from 5am to 12pm. Route 545 buses will be diverting and using different stops while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 8,000 people are expected to take part in the inaugural Parramatta Half Marathon on Sunday 21 June starting from 7am. Participants will begin their run on O'Connell St bridge and continue through Parramatta Park, around Parramatta CBD before finishing at CommBank Stadium by 11.15am.

Road closures will be in place to support the event.

  • 3am to 10.30am: roads in Western Sydney Uni campus area including sections of Fifth St and Railway St, and in Parramatta on River Rd West between James Ruse Dr and Alfred St, and sections of Noller Pde and Baludarri Dr
  • 3am to 11am: roads in Parramatta CBD including sections of Marsden St, George St, Thomas St, Phillip St, Macarthur St, Palmer St, Marist Pl, Market St, Sorrell St, Elizabeth St and surrounding local roads. Access for residents will be maintained on sections of Phillip St, George St and surrounding local lanes.
  • 3am to 11am: all roads in Parramatta Park west of Parramatta River will be closed.
  • 3am to 12 midday: roads in Parramatta CBD including O'Connell St between Victoria Rd and Argyle St, and sections of Park Pde, Alexandra Ave eastbound, Macquarie, George, Hunter and Pitt Streets, and roads in Parramatta Park east of Parramatta River.
  • The Valley Cycleway (Parramatta River foreshore shared path) will be closed to cyclists and pedestrians between 6:30am and 10:30am during the event period.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on roads in the area.

Special event clearways will be in effect between 1am and 12pm midday on sections of O'Connell St, Victoria Rd, Elizabeth St, Hunter St, Macquarie St, George St, Macarthur St, River Rd West and other local roads.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator

arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

properties.diversions:
Motorists travelling north or south of Parramatta CBD will be detoured via Wilde Ave, Smith St and Station St East only. All other key roads corridors will be closed for the event.

Access to Parramatta Westfield will remain available from the south.

Detour around Parramatta CBD via James Ruse Dr, Victoria Rd, Cumberland Hwy, and Great Western Hwy to avoid creating and being caught in traffic congestion and delays.

roads.mainStreet: O'Connell Street, Marsden St, Macarthur St

properties.otherAdvice:
Stakes Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race from 11am, last race from 4.25pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Anzac Parade, Alison Road, High Street

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Raiders at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. Kick-off at 7.30pm, full time around 9.15pm.

With thousands of fans expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 9.15pm until 9.45pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

roads.mainStreet: O'Connell Street

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A live site will be open at Allianz Stadium screening the FIFA World Cup men's Australia vs Türkiye game on Sunday. Kick off 2pm AEST.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

roads.mainStreet: Moore Park Road, Anzac Parade, Lang Road, Eastern Distributor

A free live site will be open at Cathy Freeman Park screening the FIFA World Cup men's matches live on the big screen from Sunday 14 to Friday 26 June.

  • Sunday 14 June: Australia vs Türkiye, kick off 2pm AEST
  • Saturday 20 June: USA v Australia, kick off 5am AEST
  • Friday 26 June: Paraguay v Australia, kick off 12pm AEST

Motorists should allow extra travel time on the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
Leehom at Qudos Bank Arena. Show starts at 7pm

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park during the busy evening peak. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you're driving, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Participants and volunteers receive free travel on buses and trains for travel to and from the event. Find out more or visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Changed traffic conditions will be in place for the Bay to Bay Running Festival will be held on Sunday 14 June from 6.30am between Woy Woy and Gosford.

Key roads will be closed for the event:

  • Rolling closure of Brisbane Water Drive from Brick Wharf Road to Glenrock Parade with various side local roads that intersect with Brisbane Water Drive from 6:55am to 8:10am.
  • The Boulevarde, Woy Woy between Brisbane Water Drive and Brick Wharf Road from 5:00am Saturday 13 June to 10:00am Sunday 14 June.
  • Riou Street, Gosford, southbound between Donnison Street and Central Coast Highway from 7.30am to 12.00pm.
  • Dane Drive, Gosford, between Donnison Street and Georgiana Terrace from 6.50am to 1:10pm.
  • Alfred Higgs Place, Gosford between the roundabout on Central Coast Highway to the carpark underneath Brian McGowan Bridge from 5am to 1pm. 

Motorists travelling between Gosford and Woy Woy will be diverted via Woy Woy Rd.

Motorists should avoid the area during the event but if travel is necessary, allow extra travel time and follow the directions of traffic controllers.

roads.mainStreet: Brisbane Water Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
Star Struck will be held on Friday 12 and Saturday 13 June at Newcastle Entertainment Centre.

Motorists are advised to expect delays on Griffiths Road at the entrance to the Newcastle Entertainment Centre as patrons travel to and from the event. The right hand turn from Griffiths Road into the venue will be restricted, causing delays for some motorists. Access to Chatham Road from Griffiths Road will also be restricted each day. Motorists should allow extra time and follow the directions from traffic controllers.

roads.mainStreet: Griffiths Road

properties.publicTransport: Free shuttle bus services will operate for the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL Knights v Dragons at McDonald Jones Stadium. Kick off at 8pm, full time at 10pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Griffiths Road

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport for the AFL & NRL games. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Sydney Olympic Park will be very busy on Saturday with more than 50,000 people attending various events at venues across the precinct, alongside the Supanova Comic Con & Gaming expo:

  • AFL GWS Giants v Carlton at ENGIE Stadium. First Bounce at 4.15pm, full time at 6.45pm
  • NRL Bulldogs v Sea Eagles at Accor Stadium. Kick off at 7.30pm, full time at 9.15pm
  • DAVE: The Boy Who Played the Harp tour at Qudos Bank Arena. Show starts at 8pm

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry. Parking will be in high demand with more than 50,000 people attending various events in the precinct.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
Supanova Comic Con & Gaming at Sydney Showground from Friday 19 to Sunday 21 June. Expo starts daily at 10am finishes nightly at 6pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching Sydney Olympic Park as attendees travel to and from the expo. Travel with your group and allow extra travel time on the M4, Parramatta Rd, Homebush Bay Dr and Concord Rd.

Ticketholders to Saturday’s event should expect additional traffic with 50,000 people also attending events at surrounding venues.

If you have to drive, carpool with your group pre-book your parking online ahead of time to secure a space – available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. Parking will be in high demand on with more than 50,000 people attending various events in the precinct on Saturday 20 June. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.  ﻿

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Wests Tigers v Dolphins at Campbelltown Sports Stadium. Kick-off at 3pm, full time around 5pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to the match. Allow extra travel time on roads including Pembroke Rd, Rose Payten Dr and Leumeah Rd.

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Pembroke Road and Rose Payten Drive
